title: pavonia parva
intro: Pavonia parva, also known as the small rosemallow or small swampmallow, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pavonia parva include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, tincture, and poultice, to utilize its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
